stopConditions: Stop after verified official UAB pages support narrow claim candidates.
Keep UAB Libraries guide claims scoped as guidance/positioning, not binding student policy.
Keep UAB Publications Service claims scoped to articles and editorial workflow.
Do not infer a central university-wide AI assessment rule from course guides, news pages, or source titles.

artifacts.claimText: UAB Libraries publishes a UAB positioning page on AI use in teaching that describes the document as a provisional, living document intended to support reflection on AI in university teaching rather than an exhaustive regulation.
artifacts.normalizedValue: UAB has official AI-in-teaching positioning guidance, framed as provisional and reflective

citation.citationTitle: Posicionament de la UAB respecte als usos de la IA en docencia
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: In its AI-in-teaching positioning guidance, UAB argues that ChatGPT and similar tools require rethinking assessment evidence and that tasks a machine can do quickly and well should not form part of an assessment proposal because they would evaluate artificial production.
artifacts.normalizedValue: UAB teaching guidance recommends rethinking assessments around AI-generated output

citation.citationTitle: Posicionament de la UAB respecte als usos de la IA en docencia
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UAB Libraries' responsible-use guidance for AI says privacy and data security require reviewing service terms, avoiding leakage of institutional information, and ensuring compliance with GDPR and other regulations.
artifacts.normalizedValue: UAB AI guide includes privacy, institutional data leakage, and GDPR cautions

citation.citationTitle: Aspectes etics i deontologics - Intel.ligencia Artificial - Biblioteques UAB
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UAB Libraries' AI authorship and attribution guidance says AI cannot have academic or legal authorship; the person using AI remains intellectually responsible and the AI tool should be indicated as a source, not as an author.
artifacts.normalizedValue: UAB AI guide treats AI as a tool/source, not an academic author

citation.citationTitle: Autoria i atribucio - Intel.ligencia Artificial - Biblioteques UAB
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The UAB Publications Service AI policy for articles says AI cannot be listed as an author or co-author, AI-generated scientific content requires author supervision and validation, and any AI use must be explicitly disclosed in the article.
artifacts.normalizedValue: UAB Publications Service AI policy restricts AI authorship and requires disclosure in articles

artifacts.sourceTitle: Posicionament de la UAB respecte als usos de la IA en docencia
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Aquest document n’és un primer resultat provisional. Un cop constituït el grup de treball amb professorat de diferents centres, s’ha elaborat aquest document, que serà un document viu, perquè la tecnologia a la qual es refereix evoluciona gairebé a temps real i convindrà anar-lo actualitzant regularment.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The UAB positioning document is framed as a provisional, living document that will need regular updates.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Posicionament de la UAB respecte als usos de la IA en docencia
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: La irrupció de ChatGPT i altres eines similars obliga a replantejar allò que es demana que provi o evidenciï cada estudiant. En general, tot allò que la màquina pot fer bé i de pressa no hauria de formar part de la proposta avaluativa, perquè s’estaria avaluant, en realitat, una producció artificial.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UAB's teaching guidance says ChatGPT-like tools require rethinking what assessments ask students to evidence.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section: Impacte de la IA en la docencia i l'avaluacio
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Aspectes etics i deontologics - Intel.ligencia Artificial - Biblioteques UAB
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Privacitat i seguretat de les dades. La IA es basa principalment en dades obertes d’internet, però també en informació derivada de l’ús de les pròpies eines. Cal: revisar els termes d’ús dels serveis; evitar la filtració d’informació institucional; garantir el compliment del RGPD i altres normatives.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The UAB guide says AI privacy/data security requires checking service terms, avoiding institutional-information leaks, and complying with GDPR.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section: Privacitat i seguretat de les dades
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Autoria i atribucio - Intel.ligencia Artificial - Biblioteques UAB
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: La IA no pot tenir autoria acadèmica o legal. La IA és una eina, no un subjecte amb intencionalitat ni responsabilitat. L’autoria correspon a la persona que utilitza la IA i decideix què n’incorpora. A la citació, s’indica l’eina com a font, no com a autor.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UAB Libraries says AI is a tool rather than an author, and that the person using AI remains responsible for what is incorporated.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section: Autoria i atribucio de la IA
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in articles
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The IA cannot be listed as author or co-author of an article. AI should not be used to generate hypotheses, interpret data or formulate conclusions without the supervision and validation of the authors. Failure to disclose the use of AI in the article is considered academic misconduct and may result in rejection of the proposal or retraction of the published article.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The UAB Publications Service policy restricts AI authorship, requires author supervision/validation, and treats nondisclosure as academic misconduct for articles.

Claim is supported by direct authorship and attribution language from the official UAB Libraries AI guide.

artifacts.title: Universitat Autonoma de Barcelona AI Policy Staging Notes
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source-only claim candidates for UAB. The strongest evidence is UAB Libraries guidance on AI in teaching, privacy, attribution, and authorship, plus the UAB Publications Service policy for AI use in articles. Claims are intentionally scoped to guidance or publication workflows rather than a binding university-wide student AI rule.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The UAB Libraries AI guidance pages are guidance/positioning pages and should not be read as final binding regulation.
The UAB Publications Service policy claim is scoped to articles and editorial workflow.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
